techniques.evidence_quote: discovery of a cryptominer lurking inside a Community AMI
whoever ran this AMI ... is paying for the compute
adversaries who published this AMI designed it to bill AWS customers for compute while extracting cryptocurrency
active Monero cryptominer running in one of the organization's EC2 servers

attack_chain.description: Adversary created and published a malicious Windows 2008 Server Community AMI with an embedded Monero cryptominer to the AWS Community AMI catalog.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: adversaries who published this AMI designed it to bill AWS customers for compute while extracting cryptocurrency

evidence_chunks.quote: adversaries who published this AMI designed it to bill AWS customers for compute while extracting cryptocurrency
adversaries who published this AMI designed it to bill AWS customers for compute while extracting cryptocurrency

attack_chain.description: Victim organization (a financial institution) selected and launched an EC2 instance based on the malicious Community AMI, trusting it as a legitimate Windows 2008 Server image.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: community AMIs are not verified by Amazon

evidence_chunks.quote: community AMIs are not verified by Amazon
community AMIs are not verified by Amazon

attack_chain.description: Upon launching the AMI as an EC2 instance, the preinstalled Monero cryptominer binary began executing in the background.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: cryptominer was running in it from the beginning

evidence_chunks.quote: cryptominer was running in it from the beginning
cryptominer was running in it from the beginning

attack_chain.description: The Monero miner consumed the victim's EC2 compute resources to mine cryptocurrency that was sent to the attacker's wallet, while the victim paid the AWS compute bill.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: whoever ran this AMI ... is paying for the compute, but the mined cryptocurrency goes to the attacker

evidence_chunks.quote: whoever ran this AMI ... is paying for the compute, but the mined cryptocurrency goes to the attacker
whoever ran this AMI ... is paying for the compute, but the mined cryptocurrency goes to the attacker

initial_access_vector: Victim launched a trojanized Windows 2008 Server Community AMI that contained a preinstalled Monero cryptominer.
persistence_mechanism: Cryptominer embedded within the published Community AMI image, so any instance launched from it runs the miner by default.
